messages.text: [PULSE] REST_MODE_EXPIRED: HYP-063 () validated and unimplemented for 20 days. Implement it or explicitly reject it. Validated knowledge has an expiry date.

[PULSE] OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL: 23 closed OANDA trades but 0 matched to open decisions — the win/loss loop is not closing (CLAUDE.md NON-NEGOTIABLE #2). Oracle is learning from nothing. Check pair/timestamp/system matching in decision_logger.update_outcome.

[REST-038] The recurring URGENT 'OUTCOME_LOOP_STALL: N closed OANDA trades, 0 matched' pulse is a FALSE POSITIVE — safe to ignore. The win/loss loop IS working: 2 July FOREX closes matched correctly (USD_JPY -1.0, USDJPY=X -1.002) and the 1 unmatched record is just your single still-OPEN trade. The alarm fires because pulse_check.backfill_closed_outcomes() re-fetches the last 100 closed OANDA trades every cycle and re-counts ones already back-filled (or that never had a decision); update_outcome only matches OPEN records, so resolved closes can never re-match -> 0 forever. That's why the count climbs (22->23) during a 9-day fill drought. Fix (your call, NN#4): scope the stall detector to closes whose decision is still OPEN; leave update_outcome alone, it's correct. Health: 7 loops alive, phantom USD_CAD emitter 9d dormant, no new fills since 07-03, the 07-10/11 decision-log 'gap' is benign (all pairs NO_TRADE / weekend). No code/config/ledger touched this cycle. Full: FIND-REST-038-a/b in findings.jsonl.
